commit.message:
feat: Agent Teams UI, login fix, DB migration repair, new pages (#100)

* refine: fix Agent Teams UI security, race condition, dark mode, and build env

- history.vue: Replace <a href=csvUrl> with fetch+blob download (no token in URL)
- queue.vue: Fix SSE+polling race condition; polling only starts when SSE fails
- conversations.vue: Add dark mode color variants for all agent avatars; add today's session agents
- default.vue: Fix useHead to use reactive function form for runtime env title
- nuxt.config.ts: Fix isDevelopment detection to use IAMS_BACKEND_PORT (nuxt build always sets NODE_ENV=production)

Security fixes:
- CSV export no longer puts auth token in browser URL/history
- Agent Teams colors now readable in both light and dark mode

Refined from Agent Teams session 2026-03-07

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: dark mode toggle, parallel flow graph, dev admin auto-seed

- tailwind.config.js: switch to class-based dark mode (darkMode: 'class')
- default.vue: add dark mode toggle button (🌙/☀️) in header
  - localStorage persistence across sessions
  - OS preference detection fallback on first load
  - Full dark mode styling for header, sidebar, nav items, footer
- conversations.vue: replace isDark (colorMode) with class-based detection
  - Improved flow graph: parallel branch layout using parent_id BFS traversal
  - Parallel branches render side-by-side with curved cubic Bezier edges
  - Sequential edges remain straight lines
- index.js: auto-seed admin user in dev after migrations
  - Covers both 'pending migrations' and 'RUN_MIGRATIONS=true' code paths
  - Only runs in non-production environments
  - Idempotent: skips if admin user already exists

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: builder SSE live panel, worktree page, performance nav, new sidebar items

- builder.vue: live SSE event feed with dark terminal log, progress bar,
  animated node status during simulate, SSE connection via subscribeToWorkflowEvents
- worktree.vue: new Git WorkTree page showing branch/head/commit info per tree
- default.vue: added AG パフォーマンス and AG ワークツリー to sidebar nav
- workflow.js: added GET /api/v1/workflow/worktrees endpoint (git worktree list --porcelain)
  with enriched last-commit info per worktree path

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: memory/logs UI page, builder SSE panel, worktree page, perf nav

- memory.vue: new AG Memory page with 4 tabs (ハンドオフ/実行/Agent定義/ワークフローログ)
  Stats cards, search filters, detail modal, workflow log terminal view
- workflow.js: 4 new Memory APIs:
  GET /memory/stats, /memory/logs, /memory/workflow-log, /memory/agents
- default.vue: added AG メモリ (🧠) nav item between performance and worktree
- workflow.js: extracted countLines to countJsonlLines (lint: no-inner-declarations fix)
- Both dev and prod frontends rebuilt and deployed

All todos complete: agent-trigger-ui, worktree-ui, perf-monitoring, memory-ui, kanban-backend

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add dev credentials hint to login page

Display admin/Admin@123 credentials on login screen with click-to-fill
buttons. Styled as amber warning box with '本番リリース時に削除予定' note.
Both dev (8443) and prod (9444) deployed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: admin seed double-hash — pass plain password to let beforeCreate hook hash it

The seed code was manually calling bcrypt.hash() before User.create(),
causing the password to be hashed twice (once manually, once by the
beforeCreate hook). This made login fail with AUTHENTICATION_FAILED.

Also fixed: removed stale SequelizeMeta entries that blocked missing
table migrations (cmdb_custom_role, performance_metrics, token_blacklist,
etc.) from running.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* build: update prod frontend build artifacts

Rebuilt prod frontend with latest features:
- Login credentials hint box (admin/Admin@123)
- AG Memory & Logs page (4 tabs)
- AG WorkTree browser page
- Builder SSE live panel
- New sidebar nav items (Performance, Memory, WorkTree)
- Dark mode support

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: kensan <kensan@example.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Agent: Target State Alignment (#7564)

* Agent: Target State Alignment

* Initial plan

* Enforce formatter boundary: remove collapseVertexFormatBeginSpacing from normalize-formatted-output.ts

Removes collapseVertexFormatBeginSpacing and collapseCustomFunctionToFormatEndSpacing
from the formatter's post-processing pipeline. These functions violated
target-state.md §2.1 and §3.2 by hardcoding GML API names
(vertex_format_begin, vertex_format_end) and making content-aware spacing
decisions based on those API names—a semantic/content rewrite that belongs
exclusively in @gml-modules/lint, not the formatter.

Also removes the five helper functions (stripInlineLineComment,
isSimpleFunctionCallLine, isVertexFormatEndAssignmentLine) and the two
regex patterns that only existed to serve the removed functions.

Adds a boundary test in normalize-output-formatter-boundary.test.ts that
asserts the formatter does NOT collapse blank lines around
vertex_format_begin/end calls, locking in the contract so the violation
cannot be silently re-introduced.

Co-authored-by: henrylkirk <15679826+henrylkirk@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(live-testing): add get_file_coverage MCP tool for per-line coverage

Pull-based MCP tool returning per-line coverage data for a specific file.
Uses resolveFilePath for partial path matching and projectWithCoverage for
both explicit instrumentation and synthesized fallback coverage.

JSON response includes per-line coverage status, covering test names,
branch coverage detail, and summary statistics (covered/total/percent).

- formatFileCoverageResponse: pure function for testability
- getFileCoverage: MCP tool using resolveFilePath + projectWithCoverage
- 7 tests covering all edge cases (empty, covered, not-covered, partial branch, etc.)
- Updated expected MCP tools set for registration test

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: scan all Binance Futures pairs, spot market scanner, dual market data stores, channel routing cleanup (#48)

* Initial plan

* feat: implement scanning/routing architecture from PR #47 with CI fixes

- config.py: Change AUTO_SCAN_PAIRS default to empty (scan all futures pairs)
  Add FUTURES_* batch processing and SPOT_* scanner configuration
- bot/exchange.py: Add _spot_resilient_exchange and spot helper functions
- bot/ws_manager.py: Add market_type param to MarketDataStore and WebSocketManager
  Spot uses 1h/4h/1d buffers and stream.binance.com; futures keeps 5m/15m/4h/1d
- bot/spot_scanner.py: New spot market scanner with gem detection and scam alerts
  Input validation via validate_symbol() using strict alphanumeric regex
- bot/bot.py: Add dual market data stores (futures + spot), update _broadcast to
  use TELEGRAM_CHANNEL_ID_HARD with fallback, add batch processing in fallback scan,
  add spot admin commands (/spot_scan /spot_status /scam_check)
- .env.example: Add FUTURES_* and SPOT_* env vars
- tests/test_config_sync.py: Update AUTO_SCAN_PAIRS default assertion to empty list
- tests/test_spot_scanner.py: New tests (all properly mocked, no network calls)
- tests/test_full_market_scan.py: New tests (all properly mocked)
- tests/test_separate_market_data.py: New tests for dual market data instances
- tests/test_channel_cleanup.py: New tests for channel routing (properly mocked)"

docs: achieve 80% function documentation coverage (87.97%)

Add comprehensive GoDoc comments to 18 undocumented exported symbols in testdata/
directory, exceeding the 80% function documentation coverage target by 7.97pp.

Changes:
- testdata/duplication/below_threshold.go: AuthenticateUserByPassword (250 chars)
- testdata/duplication/small_blocks.go: GetUserID, IsValidID, CheckErrorA (200-250 chars)
- testdata/naming/bad_file_name.go: ExampleFunction (200 chars)
- testdata/naming/bad_identifiers.go: UserId type (220 chars)
- testdata/placement/misplaced_function/database.go: BatchProcess, CheckUser, VerifyUser (200-250 chars)
- testdata/simple/concurrency.go: 8 concurrency pattern examples (220-260 chars each)
- testdata/simple/interfaces.go: Any type (180 chars)

Metrics:
- Function documentation coverage: 77.85% → 87.97% (+10.13pp) ✅
- Overall documentation coverage: 79.16% → 88.31% (+9.15pp)
- Zero complexity regressions in modified files
- Duplication ratio: 0.4287% → 0.4284% (-0.0003pp improvement)

All tests pass with race detection. Completes Step 5 of PLAN.md.

commit.message:
refactor: migrate JWT middleware from v2 to v3 (#400)

* feat: migrate go-jwt-middleware from v2 to v3

- Update module dependency from v2.3.1 to v3.0.0
- Migrate JWKS provider to use options pattern (WithIssuerURL, WithCacheTTL, WithCustomClient)
- Migrate validator to use options pattern (WithKeyFunc, WithAlgorithm, WithIssuer, WithAudiences)
- Update claims access pattern: use type assertion on ValidateToken return value
- Update error handling and API signatures for v3 compatibility
- Add issuerURL parameter to NewAuth() since IssuerURL is no longer exported from CachingProvider

Breaking changes in v3:
- Pure options pattern for all constructors (no positional arguments)
- ValidateToken returns (interface{}, error) with type assertion required
- NewCachingProvider now returns error as second value
- All tests pass successfully

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: update docker-compose.yml for keycloak binding

- Bind keycloak port 18080 to localhost only (127.0.0.1:18080:18080)
- Required for testing JWT middleware migration

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add comprehensive tests for NewCheckAccessToken factory function

- Test success cases: normal config, CacheTTL=0, empty ExclusionURLs
- Test error cases: nil IssuerURL, nil HTTPClient, negative CacheTTL, empty Audiences, nil Logger
- Verify typed nil handling with jwtmiddleware.Logger interface
- Use table-driven test pattern consistent with codebase style
- All test cases define complete behavior specification

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: update context helper to use go-jwt-middleware v3 for subject management and add tests

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Create complete WBS website project (Django + Vue 3 + TypeScript + Docker)

- Django 4.2 backend with DRF, CORS headers, psycopg2 (PostgreSQL)
- WBSItem model: hierarchical self-FK parent/child, status, priority,
  assignee, dates, progress (0-100), order fields
- Full CRUD REST API at /api/wbs/ via ModelViewSet + DefaultRouter
- Vue 3 + TypeScript + Vite frontend
- WBSTable: hierarchical display, colored status/priority badges, progress bars
- WBSForm: full CRUD form with parent task selector
- Axios API client with /api proxy to Django backend
- docker-compose.yml: postgres:15.5, backend:8000, frontend:3000
- Vite proxy routes /api → backend:8000 for seamless dev experience
- .gitignore: added Python, Node.js, and Docker ignores

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[ARCH] Runtime enemy JSON registration + extract Dungnz.Models (#1189, #1190) (#1198)

* feat: [ARCH] resolve JsonDerivedType circular dep and extract Dungnz.Models library (#1189, #1190)

Issue #1189 - Runtime enemy JSON registration:
- Remove all [JsonDerivedType] and [JsonPolymorphic] attributes from Enemy.cs
- Remove 'using Dungnz.Systems.Enemies' from Enemy.cs (breaks Models→Systems dep)
- Add Engine/EnemyTypeRegistry.cs: builds JsonSerializerOptions with all enemy
  derived types registered via DefaultJsonTypeInfoResolver modifier at startup
- Update Systems/SaveSystem.cs to use EnemyTypeRegistry.CreateOptions()
- Replace AllEnemySubclasses_MustHave_JsonDerivedTypeAttribute test with
  EnemyTypeRegistry_MustRegister_AllConcreteEnemySubclasses which verifies
  EnemyTypeRegistry covers every concrete Enemy subclass in the game assembly
- Update Enemy_Serialization_MatchesSnapshot to use EnemyTypeRegistry options

Issue #1190 - Extract Dungnz.Models class library:
- Move all Models/*.cs into Dungnz.Models/ (34 files, namespaces unchanged)
- Move Systems/SkillTree.cs and Systems/MerchantInventoryConfig.cs to
  Dungnz.Models/ (were already in namespace Dungnz.Models)
- Move Data/DefaultItems.cs to Dungnz.Models/ and change namespace from
  Dungnz.Data to Dungnz.Models (eliminates Models→Data circular dep)
- Remove 'using Dungnz.Data' from LootTable.cs and Merchant.cs
- Update Dungnz.Models.csproj: add GenerateDocumentationFile, align
  WarningsAsErrors with main project, add InternalsVisibleTo Dungnz and
  Dungnz.Tests
- Update Dungnz.csproj: add <Compile Remove="Models/**" />
- Update Dungnz.Tests.csproj: add direct ProjectReference to Dungnz.Models
- Update ArchitectureTests to load both GameLoop and Enemy assemblies
- Fix unresolvable cref doc comments in IDisplayService.cs, Item.cs, Player.cs

Build: 0 errors, 0 warnings. Tests: 1734/1734 passed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: update README architecture section for Models extraction (#1189, #1190)

- Replace Models/ with Dungnz.Models/ class library entry
- Add EnemyTypeRegistry.cs to Engine section
- Move MerchantInventoryConfig and SkillTree to Dungnz.Models listing
- Remove those entries from Systems listing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Merge pull request #5 from dchu3/feat/verbose-logging

feat: add --verbose flag for debug logging

Add a verbose/debug logging mode that surfaces tool call details,
MCP server responses, and errors to stderr. Enabled via:
- --verbose or -v CLI flag
- VERBOSE=true env var

This helps diagnose issues like opaque MCP server errors that Gemini
currently hides behind vague user-facing messages.

commit.message:
Merge pull request #4 from dchu3/fix/network-validation

fix: validate and normalize SOLANA_NETWORK in config

The MCP server expects "mainnet" or "devnet", but Solana RPC uses
"mainnet-beta" which is a common source of confusion. Config now:
- Normalizes "mainnet-beta" to "mainnet" automatically
- Validates the value is one of the accepted options at startup
- Fails fast with a clear error instead of a cryptic MCP server error

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

curl errors.

  default) and BT_CHURN_WINDOW (seconds, default 300) in config.json.
  Passes values through BluetoothManager constructor.

section and keeps the caution notes grouped below the quickstart flow.

commit.message:
Fix request body and response tabs merging into one tab group (#76) pymdownx.tabbed treats consecutive === blocks separated by only whitespace as a single tab group. After issue #35, the content-type tabs in request-body and the response-code tabs in request-responses were both at column 0 with only blank lines between them, causing them to merge into one tab group. Fix: add a

heading before the responses tab loop in request-responses.html. This provides a non-tab line that breaks the tab group chain, keeping request body tabs and response tabs separate. Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

"mainnet-beta" which is a common source of confusion. Config now:
- Normalizes "mainnet-beta" to "mainnet" automatically
- Validates the value is one of the accepted options at startup
- Fails fast with a clear error instead of a cryptic MCP server error

commit.message:
Add .env file support for credentials

Load TRELLO_API_KEY and TRELLO_API_TOKEN from .env files as a
complement to environment variables. Lookup order:

1. .env in the current working directory
2. ~/.config/trello/.env (fallback)

Environment variables always take priority over .env values.
The .env parser is a lightweight built-in implementation (no
external dependencies) supporting KEY=VALUE pairs, comments,
blank lines, and quoted values.

- Add DotEnv.swift: lightweight .env file parser
- Add Environment.swift: loads .env from cwd then config dir
- Integrate Environment.load() into makeClient()
- Update missingCredentials error message to mention .env
- Add TrelloCLITests target with 10 parser tests
- Update README.md and AGENTS.md documentation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(analyse): add deep analysis sections - moon phase profit, cosmic correlation, time-based performance

- AnalysePage.razor: Add 4 new analysis sections:
  * Moon Phase Profit Analysis (waxing vs waning, 8 phases with win rate/PnL)
  * Cosmic Event Correlation (cosmic score bands vs trading performance, Mercury retrograde impact)
  * Time-Based Performance (by 4-hour blocks and day of week)
  * AI Learning Insights (learning curve, top features, tech vs esoteric breakdown)
- ServiceRegistration.cs: Register AnalysisInsightsService in DI container
- AnalysisInsightsService.cs: Full implementation with:
  * Julian date moon phase calculation (synodic month algorithm)
  * Trade memory correlation with lunar phases
  * Cosmic score band analysis using FinancialAstrologyService
  * Mercury retrograde win rate comparison
  * Time-of-day and day-of-week performance breakdowns
  * AI learning curve (early vs recent win rate)
  * Auto-generated KI insights based on learned weights

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
